title: bulbinella triquetra var. trinervis
intro: Bulbinella triquetra var. trinervis, also known as the Golden-headed Onion, is a species of plant in the family Asphodelaceae. This plant is a type of bulbous perennial that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine. The Golden-headed Onion is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, and can be consumed raw or dried, although its preparation and dosage should be carefully guided by a healthcare professional.
